title: Mercury-Atlas 7 / Aurora 7 (SEQUENCE BREAKER)
description: Open datasets and source materials for Mercury-Atlas 7 / Aurora 7 (1962), Oplopanax horridus (Devil's Club), Red-shafted Flicker (Colaptes auratus cafer), and reentry error propagation

sources.name: NSSDC Master Catalog — Mercury-Atlas 7
sources.description: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive entry for Mercury-Atlas 7 (Aurora 7). Spacecraft description, orbital parameters, experiment manifest, and mission timeline. Designation 1962-019A.
sources.mission_relevance: Definitive source for Aurora 7 spacecraft specifications, orbital elements, and mission outcomes including the 402 km splashdown overshoot.

sources.name: NASA SP-6 — Results of the Second US Manned Orbital Space Flight
sources.description: Complete official mission report for Mercury-Atlas 7, including trajectory reconstruction, retrofire anomaly analysis, fuel consumption timeline, and scientific experiment results.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary technical documentation for the retrofire error propagation analysis, fuel budget reconstruction, and firefly ice crystal investigation results.

sources.name: NASA Technical Reports Server — Project Mercury
sources.description: Full-text technical report archive for the Mercury program, including spacecraft systems performance, flight dynamics, and operational procedures.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides engineering context for all Mercury flights, enabling comparison of Carpenter's fuel management with Glenn (MA-6) and Schirra (MA-8).

sources.name: USDA PLANTS Database — Oplopanax horridus
sources.description: Distribution maps, habitat data, and taxonomic information for Devil's Club (Oplopanax horridus) across North America.
sources.mission_relevance: Establishes the geographic range and habitat requirements of the organism pairing, documenting its PNW distribution from Alaska to Oregon.

sources.name: Flora of North America — Oplopanax horridus
sources.description: Taxonomic description of Oplopanax horridus including morphology, spine characteristics, leaf dimensions, and berry cluster structure.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides the detailed morphological data for the organism pairing — spine dimensions, leaf sizes, and growth habit that inform the resonance axes.

sources.name: Xeno-canto — Northern Flicker Recordings
sources.description: Field recordings of Northern Flicker (Colaptes auratus) vocalizations and drumming from across North America, including Red-shafted (cafer) subspecies from the Pacific Northwest.
sources.mission_relevance: Audio source material for the flicker drumming sound design artifact and acoustic analysis of the SPS species' ~25 beats/second territorial drumming.

sources.name: Macaulay Library (Cornell) — Colaptes auratus
sources.description: Research-grade audio and video recordings of Northern Flicker behavior, drumming, and vocalizations from the Cornell Lab of Ornithology.
sources.mission_relevance: High-quality audio for spectral analysis of flicker drumming rate and amplitude envelope, used in the sound design specifications.

sources.name: NASA Image and Video Library — Mercury Program
sources.description: Historical photographs from the Mercury-Atlas 7 mission: spacecraft assembly, Atlas on LC-14, Carpenter in the capsule, recovery operations, and the overshoot search.
sources.mission_relevance: Visual documentation of the Aurora 7 spacecraft, the launch, and the recovery — essential for Blender animation, Minecraft build, and visual narrative artifacts.

sources.name: Tuskegee University Archives — Booker T. Washington Papers
sources.description: Primary documents from Booker T. Washington's tenure at Tuskegee Institute, including correspondence, speeches, photographs, and institutional records.
sources.mission_relevance: Source material for the dedication connecting Washington's practical education philosophy to the College of Knowledge's Learn → Practice → Create → Contribute pathway.

sources.name: Native American Ethnobotany Database — Oplopanax horridus
sources.description: Database of Indigenous uses of devil's club across North American nations, compiled from ethnobotanical literature. Documents 38+ nations' uses for medicine, ceremony, and material culture.
sources.mission_relevance: Comprehensive documentation of Oplopanax horridus ethnobotanical significance, supporting the organism pairing's central resonance between medicinal value and defensive armament.
